item.title: When things go wrong. The following collection contains people doing stupid things, people getting very unlucky, people getting very angry and a high content of schadenfreude.
item.description:
No one was seriously injured in any of these, at least that I can tell. Enjoy!
I love this one so much. He never sees the squirrel. For him the customer opens the door, screams in terror and slams the door in his face. It's beautiful.
Remember folks, for a kitchen fire, put a lid on it or use an appropriate fire extinguisher.
Let's finish off with a win.
item.dc:creator: PackratMagpie

item.title: Delighted for her!! As someone who has a similar anniversary, can I just say reach out. Your brain is lying, you will get well and you will feel happiness again. Please open up to someone, they care!

This is the Gulf War vet who famously coined the term "Cadet Bone Spurs' for Trump, among other gems like:
Tammy's Wiki: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tammy_Duckworth

item.title: Things seen this week during structural assessments.
item.description:
This week has been heartbreaking for countless families and entire communities. We are devastated by the widespread destruction caused by the wildfires sweeping through the Greater Los Angeles area, impacting hundreds of thousands of Angelenos. And although we are still facing one of the worst fires in California history, we have seen incredible unity as communities come together and support one another. We want to sincerely thank the thousands of firefighters not just from Los Angeles but from other states, the National Guard, and other countries that have put their lives at risk.
Smoke filling the entire sky. The Palisades and Eaton fires have collectively destroyed over 12,000 homes, businesses, schools, and other structures since they began last Tuesday.
The strong winds that contributed to the spread of the fire brought down countless trees, including this massive branch that crashed onto two cars.
The intense winds reached speeds of 50-80 mph, with some areas experiencing up to 100 mph in the mountains. Here is another tree down, crushing two more cars beneath its weight.
This video was taken by one of our Structural Assessors, Markian, whose Topanga home was saved by a helicopter water drop.
This is from the Eaton Fire in Altadena. Many homes were engulfed in flames and burned to the ground. The devastation left this place nearly unrecognizable.
Our team immediately began working with the Vice Mayor of Pasadena to handle anything on his radar with the resources we have, regardless of the scope. His primary concern was exit roads for evacuation, so we cleared roads, be it removing a massive 100-year-old downed cypress tree blocking 3,000 homes, or just clearing and hauling branches and brush.
The destruction and loss are hard to believe, and seeing our community lose everything has been hard to accept. Shoutout to our team who always responds, no matter what or when. We are currently providing trucks and crews to help property owners move salvageable belongings, cut hazardous trees, create property access, and more. Resources are limited, but we’ll do our best to assist. Email us at info@alphastructural.com with pictures of the damage, your property address, and contact information and we will respond as fast as we can. Our phone lines are open as well for free guidance on engineering questions, insurance claims, legal resources, and rebuilding consulting. Stay strong LA. We will make it through this as we ALWAYS do.
item.dc:creator: AlphaStructural

item.title: Leon always tries to take credit for others ideas
item.description: Even his fascist leader
item.dc:creator: ssumner13

item.title: Remember Jack Smith
item.description:
The Memorial to the Murdered Members of the Reichstag in Berlin is dedicated to the 96 members of the German congress who spoke out against Hitler, but failed to stop him as he thundered into power in 1933.
These were the last people who could have stopped the fascist dictator…so they became his first victims. Each slate slab remembers one politician, with his name, political party, and the date and location of his death (generally in a concentration camp). They are honored in front of the building — Germany’s capitol — where they worked to defend democracy in their country.
item.dc:creator: arewenotkingsandqueenstogether

item.title: Another year, another reprimanding..
item.description: Just had my yearly performance review. Got told off for excessive "gallows humour" and banned from saying "big mad" in the teams comms.... Apparently someone was triggered that i was winding them up, saying that oh yeah such and such person will be big mad if you fk that up... I'm surrounded by morons and narcs

item.title: Tuesd'y Dumpy Doo
item.description:
I remember it like it was yesterday
Alice Cooper? Ugliest woman I've ever seen.
H. Jon doesn't look that much different
I'd swing by the fountain a time or two when I was feelin' froggy
I'm a 43 year old boy
Got it.
MUFASA AIN'T GOT NOTHIN' ON ME
Kids today don't even KNOW
wibbly wobbly, timey wimey
"look at my new necklace"
They're going to need him to come in on Saturday
The cameo guest appearance on Tulsa King was the last nail in the coffin for that show. I love Stallone, but this show is not my cup of tea.
Wrangler butts drive me nuts
It's just a bit o' Marjory Stewart-Baxter, or whatever you crazy Brits would call it
I for one actually prefer Waffle House to IHOP.
The machines are trying to kill us!
"Hope the squash does better this year. Last year was too hot!"
WHY AM I NOT ASLEEP YET???
At last, their time has come
Those bugs all look the same
Old vs Nu
Then he waddled away (waddle waddle)
The most important meal of the day
Can we eat it while we drive?
yes.
item.dc:creator: ddubya42

item.title: Part... whatever of releasing memes back to the wild
item.description:
1. The Spoom!
2. They were so caught up in whether they *could*, they never stopped to think if they *should*
3. I showed this to my wife and she got mad at me
4. "We have Oroborous at home"
5. Lol someone's gonna get mad
6. GULp
7. "Optional"
8. I'll never not find the screaming cowboy funny
9. Someone is gonna "akchyully" about various types of raptors in the comments, while missing the meme entirely
10.
11.
12.
13. I love how no matter how old this is, it's still equally relevant
14. There's more to this, but it paints the picture well
15. I don't do the 1st one only because it would be difficult on a keyboard
16. Not sure why they included him and Peter dinklage, but I'll allow it
17. The ADHD impulse to have all 3 simultaneously and be covered
18. For Karl! Rock n' Stone to the bone!
19. I can't wait to get whacked again... it's been so long...
20. If you know, you know
21.
22.
23.
24.
25. Guilty
26. "Akchuyally" incoming about either them being Maia, or about which one came to middle earth 1st (or the fact that they came to middle earth once before)
27.
28.
29.
30.
31.
32.
33.
34. He can't keep getting away with this!
35.
36.
37.
38.
39.
40.
41. They finally found antifa
42.
43.
44. Coke milk
45.
46.
47. Will always be funny
48. This is what I always thought "give your all" meant
49.
50.
item.dc:creator: GeneralSica

I’ve added “omg thalpius stop ittt” into my lexicon
Fernanda Suarez
George O’Connor
George O’Connor
George O’Connor “Apollo you old so and so!”
I love Hadestown so much it’s my ringtone
I don’t like how I think this is implying Apollo and Artemis are a thing- but when has that stopped anyone is myth. I don’t like it because Artemis is obviously a lesbian.

item.title: Whenever I’m trying to make a video, annoying human in the back trying to get all the attention
item.dc:creator: tampacl

item.title: Worked in a library for 10 years. Can confirm libraries get weird.
item.description: So two memorable stories from when I worked in university library on campus years ago: 1) First one isn't so much a story as it is an observation. But over the years while working there I would occasionally find that someone had watered some of the potted plants with BBQ sauce. No idea why or what that was about. 2) One day while using the restroom at work I was washing my hands when a guy bursts through the door and makes a dash for the stall, panting the whole way. My first thought was it was a good thing I had just gotten out of the stall, otherwise he might have had an accident. My second thought moments later was that I was about to be in school shooting. The reason for that is because maybe a minute after he entered the stall I heard what honestly sounded like a shotgun being fired into the toilet bowl. While I don't own any firearms, I have used shotguns before for target shooting. So I know what one sounds like and how loud they are. I stood there at the sink wondering if maybe my brain was playing a trick on me or if I should run. But I just stood there for a few moments waiting to see what happened so I could know for sure I had really heard what I did. But nothing happened. I kept waiting, wondering if this guy had snuck a sawed off in there under his coat or in a pant leg and was just prepping it for his rampage. Finally I peeked around the partition wall blocking view of the stall and urinals from the door and saw the guy's feet planted on the floor in front of the toilet like he was sitting there. Absolutely no signs of damage to the toilet or stall from what I could see. So I start mentally questioning what I had just heard moments ago. Then the smell hit me and I realized with horror what I had just heard. What I had heard was the sound of a large man blowing out his colon with enough force to be as loud as a shotgun. I briefly considered asking him if he needed me to call anyone to help him, but the smell quickly became overwhelming and I decided minimum wage was not enough to stay in there and deal with that, so I took off. (Apparently he was fine because he was gone later and no paramedics or police or anything showed up to check on him.)
item.dc:creator: Othagovna

item.title: PSA: Be safe out there!
item.description: Some idiot I know (definitely not me!) couldn’t wait a couple hours for the embers in the wood stove to burn out, and I, I mean he, managed to vacuum up some sparks and set fire to the filter in my, I mean his, shop vac.
item.dc:creator: RadioactiveNerfDart
